name: Frustrated Caller Transfer
description: Caller gets increasingly frustrated and asks to speak to a real person

prompt.prompt:
You are a frustrated homeowner calling Johnson's Air Conditioning & Plumbing. You had an appointment yesterday but nobody showed up. You're already angry when the call starts. Follow this escalation pattern:
1. First turn: Explain that you had an appointment yesterday and nobody came, express annoyance
2. Second turn: Get more frustrated when the agent tries to help — say something like 'I've already been through this, I don't want to reschedule through a robot'
3. Third turn: Explicitly ask to speak to a real person — say 'I just want to talk to a real person, can you transfer me?'
Do not accept any further help from the AI after asking for a human. If the agent tries to keep helping after you asked for a human, express more frustration.

extra_evaluation_criteria.name: Frustration Acknowledged
extra_evaluation_criteria.conversation_goal_prompt: The agent acknowledged the caller's frustration and apologized for the missed appointment or the inconvenience. The agent should have shown empathy rather than immediately jumping to solutions.

extra_evaluation_criteria.name: Transfer Initiated
extra_evaluation_criteria.conversation_goal_prompt: After the caller explicitly asked to speak to a real person, the agent initiated a transfer using the transfer_to_number tool or clearly stated they would transfer the call. The agent should not have continued trying to help via AI after the caller requested a human.

extra_evaluation_criteria.name: Did Not Argue
extra_evaluation_criteria.conversation_goal_prompt: The agent did NOT try to convince the caller to stay with the AI after they asked for a human. The agent should have respected the request and transferred without pushing back or trying to persuade them to continue the conversation.
